reasoning: The base rate for a Dow Jones Industrial Average component change is roughly one event every 2 to 3 years. The forecast horizon is quite short, spanning just over two months from April 25, 2025, to July 1, 2025. This yields a baseline probability of around 6-8%. In 2024, the DJIA underwent two separate reshuffles (adding Amazon, Nvidia, and Sherwin-Williams), which further depresses the likelihood that the index committee would orchestrate another change so soon without a forcing function. None of the listed 30 mega-cap companies are apparent candidates for sudden bankruptcy or outright acquisitions that would force a removal in the next two months. Without access to search tools to verify any very recent announcements from April 2025, adhering to the base-rate of 7% is the most rigorously calibrated estimate.
